mapping.a_element: quantum critical point plus defect potential strength tuning the system to criticality
mapping.b_element: threshold of brane-black-hole formation, the control parameter tuning to the critical solution

mapping.a_element: universal slowly-decaying spatial tail of the nucleated order-parameter droplet
mapping.b_element: self-similar scaling of solutions in the neighborhood of the critical (Choptuik-type) solution

brief_justification: Both merely invoke the generic 'critical threshold plus scaling' framework, but the concrete machinery differs: paper A is a static defect that nucleates a (mean-field, at/above the upper critical dimension) order-parameter droplet with a power-law spatial tail, its physics centered on dissipative channels suppressing quantum tunneling, whereas paper B is a self-similar Choptuik-type gravitational critical collapse at the topology-changing threshold between a horizon-crossing and a non-crossing brane. There is no static defect-nucleated droplet or dissipation mechanism in B and no self-similar collapse or two-phase merger of the object itself in A, so no tight role-for-role mapping of the driving processes holds.
